title: Command Packaging, Build Identity, Cross-Platform Behavior, and Distribution
description: The binary works only from the repository root, embeds no version identity, writes beside itself, assumes Unix signals and paths, and ships an untested archive from a dirty tree. Learners cumulatively produce reproducible cross-platform Go Pokedex artifact through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Keep cmd entrypoint thin, internal packages directional, fixtures separate, and runtime paths independent of source checkout.
Expose semantic version, revision, build time policy, Go version, platform, and dirty-state evidence without making builds unnecessarily nondeterministic.
Test path, newline, signal, terminal, rename, permission, and architecture assumptions on supported platforms and document differences.
